2010	theorem pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange
2011	    {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
2012	    IsIso ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
2013	      (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2014	        (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2015	        (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2016	        (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M))) := by
2017	  -- Assemble the chain (blueprint `lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`): the domain
2018	  -- read `Θ_src` (`base_change_mate_domain_read`) and codomain read `Θ_tgt`
2019	  -- (`base_change_mate_codomain_read`, resting on the `pullbackSpecIso` leg identification
2020	  -- `pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`) identify `Γ(α)`'s domain/codomain with `R' ⊗_R M` and
2021	  -- `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M`; the generator trace `base_change_mate_generator_trace` shows the conjugate
2022	  -- `Θ_src⁻¹ ≫ Γ(α) ≫ Θ_tgt` is an isomorphism (it is `cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, no flatness).
2023	  -- Conjugating back, `Γ(α)` is itself an isomorphism.
2024	  haveI hconj := base_change_mate_generator_trace ψ φ M
2025	  set D := base_change_mate_domain_read ψ φ M with hD
2026	  set C := base_change_mate_codomain_read ψ φ M with hC
2027	  set Γα := (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).map
2028	    (pushforwardBaseChangeMap (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
2029	      (Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2030	      (Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ))
2031	      (IsPullback.of_hasPullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)).w (tilde M)) with hΓα
2032	  have heq : Γα = D.hom ≫ (D.inv ≫ Γα ≫ C.hom) ≫ C.inv := by
2033	    simp [Category.assoc]
2034	  rw [heq]
2035	  infer_instance

2037	/-! ## The affine base-change lemma and its locality reduction -/
2038	
2039	/-- **Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map (locality reduction).** For `f` affine
2040	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
2041	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is
2042	the locality reduction underlying `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: it discharges the
2043	affine-open locality criterion `Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens` for the base-change map,
2044	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)
2045	by the section-level computation. The `[IsAffineHom f]`/`[F.IsQuasicoherent]` hypotheses are
2046	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
2047	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
2048	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
2049	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2050	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
2051	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
2052	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
2053	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H
2054	
2055	/-- **Affine base change.** If `f` is an affine morphism and the square is
2056	cartesian, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an isomorphism. In the
2057	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
2058	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
2059	
2060	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
2061	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
2062	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2063	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
2064	  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
2065	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
2066	  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine–affine
2067	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
2068	  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
2069	  intro U
2070	  -- Remaining goal: `IsIso (Hom.app (pushforwardBaseChangeMap …) U)` for `U` affine.
2071	  --
2072	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
2073	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
2074	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
2075	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
2076	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
2077	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
2078	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
2079	  --
2080	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
2081	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
2082	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
2083	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
2084	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
2085	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
2086	  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
2087	  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the
2088	  -- tilde–Γ counit isos to pass `IsIso Γ(α) ⟹ IsIso α` on the QC chart). This restriction-
2089	  -- compatibility of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is itself Mathlib-absent and is the remaining
2090	  -- multi-hundred-LOC build for the unconditional general theorem.
2091	  -- See `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.
2092	  sorry

2094	/-- **Flat base change, `i = 0` case.** If `g` is flat and `f` is quasi-compact
2095	and quasi-separated, then the base-change map for the pushforward is an
2096	isomorphism. Equivalently, in the affine situation `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2097	with `A → B` flat, the comparison map `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B → H⁰(X_B, F_B)` is an
2098	isomorphism.
2099	
2100	Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH ("Flat base change"), the `i = 0` case. -/
2101	theorem flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [Flat g]
2102	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
2103	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
2104	  -- Proof strategy (Stacks 02KH, `i = 0`), deferred to a later iteration:
2105	  -- the statement is local on `S'`, so reduce to `S = Spec A`, `S' = Spec B`
2106	  -- with `A → B` flat.  Choose a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`.  Since `f`
2107	  -- is quasi-compact and quasi-separated the Čech complex of `𝒰` computes
2108	  -- `H⁰(X, F)`, and base change identifies `Čech(𝒰_B, F_B) ≅ Čech(𝒰, F) ⊗_A B`
2109	  -- term by term via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`.  Flatness of `A → B`
2110	  -- makes `- ⊗_A B` exact, so it commutes with `H⁰`, giving the isomorphism
2111	  -- `H⁰(X, F) ⊗_A B ≅ H⁰(X_B, F_B)`.  Needs the (missing) Čech-cohomology /
2112	  -- affine-cover infrastructure for `SheafOfModules`; see
2113	  -- `informal/affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso.md`.

2092	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,

2096	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs} at the literal pullback projection legs
2097	  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\): with the codomain read

2099	  those legs, the concrete statement follows directly. The remainder of this proof records the
2100	  mathematical content of that abstract identity.

2102	  Throughout, let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim} \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the
2103	  canonical isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}, and
2104	  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
2105	  inclusions, so that by Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} the two projection legs
2106	  factor as
2107	  \[
2108	    g' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A,
2109	    \qquad
2110	    f' \;=\; e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'} .
2111	  \]

2113	  \[
2114	    f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr) \;\circ\;
2115	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g', f})^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2116	    (\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}} \;\circ\;
2117	    (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f', g})^{\mathrm{inv}},
2118	  \]
2119	  with \(f = \operatorname{Spec}\varphi\), \(g = \operatorname{Spec}\psi\),
2120	  \(\eta^{g'}\) the unit of the \((g')\)-adjunction, and
2121	  \(w : g' \circ f = f' \circ g\) the commutativity of the square.
2122	
2123	  \emph{Why a direct substitution fails.} The identity cannot be obtained by substituting the
2124	  leg factorizations directly, because the legs \(g'\) and \(f'\) appear in multiple positions
2125	  simultaneously: as the index of the adjunction \(\eta^{g'}\), inside the equality proof
2126	  \(w\) parametrizing \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w\), inside the argument
2127	  \(f'_{*}\bigl((g')^{*}\widetilde M\bigr)\) of the codomain comparison
2128	  \(\gamma_{\psi}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), and inside the opaque codomain
2129	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
2130	  The reindex is therefore restructured into three sub-steps so that the legs become explicit
2131	  free variables; it is \emph{not} a single rewrite.
2132	
2133	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
2134	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2135	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2136	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2137	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2138	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2139	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2140	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2141	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2142	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2143	
2144	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2145	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2146	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2147	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2148	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2149	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2150	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2151	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2152	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2153	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2154	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2155	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2156	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2157	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2158	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2159	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2160	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2161	  sections.
2162	
2163	  \emph{(iii) Reduction to Seam~1 via the leg-reindex engine.} With the legs substituted
2164	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
2165	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),
2166	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), rewrites the unit factor
2167	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the
2168	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})\) coherence, into the affine
2169	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the
2170	  \(e\)-unit. Since \(e\) is an isomorphism of schemes, its pullback functor is an equivalence
2171	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an
2172	  isomorphism absorbed into the codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). The surviving
2173	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit has section value over \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) equal to
2174	  the algebraic unit \(\eta_M : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Seam~1
2175	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}). Composing the surviving factor with the
2176	  codomain identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\)
2177	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}) and reading over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) by
2178	  restriction of scalars along \(\psi\) identifies all four factors with the restriction of
2179	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely
2180	  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\)
2181	  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}) — the
2182	  ``\(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), \(\mathcal F'\) is
2183	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
2184	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.
2185	\end{proof}

2637	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2638	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2639	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2640	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2641	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2642	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2643	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2644	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2645	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2646	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2647	  \[
2648	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2649	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2650	  \]
2651	\end{lemma}
2652	\begin{proof}
2653	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2654	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2655	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2656	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2657	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2658	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2659	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2660	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2661	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2662	\end{proof}

3019	  \(\psi : R \to R'\), \(f = \operatorname{Spec}(\varphi)\) for \(\varphi : R \to A\),
3020	  and \(M\) an \(A\)-module — write \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) for the global-sections
3021	  incarnation of the base-change map (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}).
3022	  Recall \(\alpha\) runs
3023	  \(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}) \to f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M}\). Transport \(\Gamma(\alpha)\)
3024	  through the two proved affine dictionaries that identify its domain and codomain: the
3025	  pushforward dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}, followed by the
3026	  pullback dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, identifies the domain
3027	  \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M})\bigr)\) with \(R' \otimes_R M\); the pullback
3028	  dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, followed by the pushforward
3029	  dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}, identifies the codomain
3030	  \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M}\bigr)\) with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\),
3031	  both read as \(R'\)-modules. Under these identifications \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is
3032	  precisely the canonical cancellation isomorphism
3033	  \[
3034	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
3035	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
3036	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
3037	  \]
3038	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}; here \(a \cdot m\) is the \(A\)-action on \(M\)). Equivalently, in the direction in which
3039	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is presented — from the source \(R' \otimes_R M\) to the target
3040	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) — it is \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}^{-1}\),
3041	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\); the two readings agree because
3042	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism. Since
3043	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with \emph{no} flatness
3044	  hypothesis, this identification closes the affine case: \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an
3045	  isomorphism, hence (full faithfulness / counit isomorphism of \(\widetilde{(-)}\) on
3046	  quasi-coherent modules) so is \(\alpha\) on the affine chart.
3047	
3048	\end{lemma}

3052	        lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace,
3053	        lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
3054	  The proof chains the four pieces into which the generator trace has been
3055	  decomposed. By Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_domain_read} the
3056	  source \(\Gamma\bigl(g^*(f_*\widetilde M)\bigr)\) is
3057	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) via \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}\); by
3058	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} — which rests on
3059	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} identifying the cone legs, the single
3060	  Mathlib-absent step —
3061	  the target \(\Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)\) is
3062	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) via \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). By the generator
3063	  trace Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_generator_trace} the conjugated map
3064	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}\) sends
3065	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). By the inverse formula of
3066	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib} this assignment is exactly
3067	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}^{-1}\); equivalently, in the
3068	  cancellation direction \((r' \otimes a) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes (a \cdot m)\) is
3069	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\).
3070	
3071	  Since \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is a Mathlib linear equivalence carrying
3072	  \emph{no} flatness hypothesis, the conjugated map is an isomorphism; being the
3073	  conjugate of \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) by the two dictionary isomorphisms
3074	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}, \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\), it follows that
3075	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is itself an isomorphism. This closes the affine case

3078	  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} \circ \Gamma(\alpha) \circ \Theta_{\mathrm{src}}^{-1}

3080	  same chain once \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}, \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) are formed; the
3081	  formalized decl records the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\ \Gamma(\alpha)\) corollary, which is
3082	  what the affine close consumes.)

3141	% NOTE: This affine lemma is closed directly on global sections, via the two named
3142	% obligations: lem:base_change_map_affine_local (locality reduction on the affine
3143	% opens of S') and lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange (the section-
3144	% level value of the base-change map is cancelBaseChange). Both proved affine tilde
3145	% dictionaries -- lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso and lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso --
3146	% feed the second obligation. cancelBaseChange is in Mathlib with no flatness; the

3148	\begin{proof}
3149	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:base_change_map_affine_local, lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}
3150	  The proof is the composite of the two named obligations, taken \emph{directly on
3151	  global sections}.
3152	
3153	  \medskip
3154	  \emph{First reduction (locality on \(S'\)).} By
3155	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
3156	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
3157	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
3158	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
3159	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
3160	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
3161	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to
3162	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict
3163	  to \(\widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\). (It is here that quasi-coherence is
3164	  used: it is what licenses writing \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\).) Computing the
3165	  fibre product gives \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\) and
3166	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the tilde of the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module
3167	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The matter is thus reduced to the affine--affine
3168	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
3169	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.
3170	
3171	  \medskip
3172	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
3173	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
3174	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image
3175	  the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently the counit
3176	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
3177	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are
3178	  quasi-coherent, so both counits are isomorphisms and, by their naturality,
3179	  \[
3180	    \mathrm{IsIso}\,\alpha
3181	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
3182	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Gamma(\alpha)\bigr),
3183	  \]
3184	  where \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is a concrete \(R'\)-linear map in
3185	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). The whole computation therefore stays at the level
3186	  of modules.
3187	
3188	  \medskip
3189	  \emph{Section-level identification (the second obligation).} By the section-level
3190	  computation Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, the map
3191	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\), with its domain \(\Gamma(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}))\) identified with
3192	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) and its codomain \(\Gamma(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M})\) identified
3193	  with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) through the two dictionaries
3194	  Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso},
3195	  \emph{is} the canonical cancellation isomorphism
3196	  \[
3197	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
3198	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
3199	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
3200	  \]
3201	  of \(R'\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). This is the entire
3202	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
3203	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
3204	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
3205	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
3206	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
3207	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
3208	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
3209	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).
3210	\end{proof}

3212	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
3213	
3214	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
3215	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
3216	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.

3218	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
3219	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
3220	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
3221	  \mathlibok
3222	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3223	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
3224	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
3225	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
3226	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
3227	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
3228	  \[
3229	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
3230	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
3231	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
3232	  \]
3233	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
3234	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
3235	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
3236	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
3237	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
3238	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
3239	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
3240	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
3241	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
3242	\end{lemma}

3244	\begin{lemma}[Sheaf condition as an equalizer of products]
3245	  \label{lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}
3246	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts}
3247	  \mathlibok
3248	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3249	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf with values in a category with products on a
3250	  topological space, and let \(\mathcal{U} = \{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) be a family of
3251	  opens with union \(U = \bigcup_i U_i\). Then \(\mathcal{F}\) satisfies the sheaf
3252	  condition on \(\mathcal{U}\) if and only if the fork
3253	  \[
3254	    \mathcal{F}(U)
3255	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\mathrm{res}\;}\;
3256	    \prod_i \mathcal{F}(U_i)
3257	    \;\underset{\mathrm{rightRes}}{\overset{\mathrm{leftRes}}{\rightrightarrows}}\;
3258	    \prod_{(i,j)} \mathcal{F}(U_i \cap U_j)
3259	  \]
3260	  is a limit (an equalizer), where the two parallel maps restrict the \(U_i\)- and
3261	  \(U_j\)-components to \(U_i \cap U_j\). This is Mathlib's
3262	  \texttt{isSheaf\_iff\_isSheafEqualizerProducts} together with the fork
3263	  \texttt{SheafConditionEqualizerProducts.fork}; it is exactly Čech degree
3264	  \(0 \to 1\) and requires no separatedness.

3267	\begin{lemma}\leanok
3268	[Finite affine cover with quasi-compact overlaps]
3269	  \label{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3270	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact}
3271	  A quasi-compact scheme \(X\) admits a \emph{finite} affine open cover
3272	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\). If moreover \(X\) is
3273	  quasi-separated, then each pairwise intersection \(U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j\) is
3274	  quasi-compact; and if \(X\) is separated, each \(U_{ij}\) is in fact affine.
3275	  \begin{proof}
3276	    The affine opens form a basis, so \(X\) is covered by affine opens; quasi-compactness
3277	    extracts a finite subcover \(U_1, \dots, U_t\) (by Mathlib's affine cover and the
3278	    finite-subcover property of a quasi-compact space). Quasi-separatedness says the
3279	    intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact, so each \(U_{ij}\) is
3280	    quasi-compact. When \(X\) is separated the diagonal is a closed immersion, whence
3281	    the intersection of two affine opens is affine (Mathlib, for separated schemes).
3282	  \end{proof}
3283	\end{lemma}

3353	\subsection{The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear equalizer presentation (FBC-B build-ahead)}
3354	
3355	The separated-case flat base change Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated} can be organised by
3356	presenting \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\) as an honest \(\operatorname{LinearMap.eqLocus}\) over the global
3357	ring \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and invoking the Mathlib base-change-of-equalizer equivalence
3358	\(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\) (recorded as the dependency anchor
3359	Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}, which pins exactly
3360	\texttt{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}). This sub-lane is independent of the affine section-level
3361	computation; its three ingredients are recorded here as build-ahead blocks. The Lean pins below name
3362	to-be-built declarations.

3364	\begin{lemma}[$A$-module structure on cover sections by restriction of scalars]
3365	  \label{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}
3366	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaCoverRestrictScalars}
3367	  \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
3368	  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme, \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\), and write
3369	  \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) for the ring of global sections of the structure sheaf. For a finite
3370	  affine cover \(\{U_i\}\) with overlaps \(U_{ij}\), the structure-sheaf restriction ring maps
3371	  \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and
3372	  \(A \to \Gamma(U_{ij},\mathcal{O}_X)\) make each module of sections \(\Gamma(U_i, M)\),
3373	  \(\Gamma(U_{ij}, M)\) an \(A\)-module by restriction of scalars; moreover the restriction maps of
3374	  \(M\) along inclusions of opens are \(\Gamma(X,-)\)-semilinear over those restriction ring maps,
3375	  hence \(A\)-linear after restriction of scalars.
3376	  \begin{proof}
3377	    \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
3378	    Each \(\Gamma(U_i,M)\) is a module over \(\Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\); restricting scalars along
3379	    the ring map \(A \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) gives the \(A\)-module structure, and likewise on
3380	    \(U_{ij}\). A presheaf restriction of \(M\) over an inclusion \(V \subseteq U\) is additive and

3382	    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)- and \(M\)-restrictions are compatible with the maps out of the global sections
3383	    \(A\), the restriction becomes \(A\)-linear once scalars are restricted to \(A\).
3384	  \end{proof}

3454	  \operatorname{Spec} B\), \(\mathcal{F}_B\) the pullback, and

3456	  \(X_B\), and the sheaf-condition fork of Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} for
3457	  \((X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\) is obtained from that for \((X, \mathcal{F})\) by applying
3458	  \(- \otimes_A B\): there are compatible isomorphisms
3459	  \[

3461	    \qquad

3472	    \(\{(U_i)_B\}\) covers \(X_B\); since \(X\) is separated each \(U_{ij}\), hence each
3473	    \((U_{ij})_B\), is affine, and the fork of
3474	    Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} applies to \((X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\). On each
3475	    affine term the global-sections form of the affine lemma
3476	    Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward} supplies the isomorphism
3477	    \(\Gamma((U_i)_B,\mathcal{F}_B) \cong \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B\)

3497	  separated paragraph.}

3499	  \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent. Then the comparison map
3500	  \[
3501	    \Gamma(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\longrightarrow\; \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
3502	  \]
3503	  is an isomorphism.

3506	          lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}

3509	    \(\mathcal{U}\) and its base change \(\mathcal{U}_B\). By
3510	    Lemma~\ref{lem:base_changed_equalizer_diagram} the \(X_B\)-fork is the

3520	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change, Mayer--Vietoris reduction of the quasi-separated case]
3521	  \label{lem:flat_base_change_mayer_vietoris}
3522	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_mayerVietoris}
3523	  \uses{lem:flat_base_change_separated, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3524	        lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3525	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH ($i=0$ case), quasi-separated paragraph
3526	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.md; quoted verbatim in the
3527	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF of thm:flat_base_change_pushforward below).
3528	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In case $X$ is quasi-separated, choose an affine open
3529	  % covering $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$. ... The reader who
3530	  % wishes to avoid this spectral sequence can use Mayer-Vietoris and induction
3531	  % on $t$ as in the proof of Lemma
3532	  % \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}."
3533	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), $i=0$ case,
3534	  quasi-separated paragraph (Mayer--Vietoris induction on \(t\)).}
3535	  Let \(A \to B\) be flat, \(X\) a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme over \(A\),
3536	  and \(\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent. Then the comparison map
3537	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to \Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\) is an
3538	  isomorphism.
3539	  \begin{proof}
3540	    \uses{lem:flat_base_change_separated, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3541	          lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib, lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3542	    Choose a finite affine cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\)
3543	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}) and induct on \(t\). For \(t = 1\),
3544	    \(X = U_1\) is affine and the claim is the affine lemma
3545	    Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}. For \(t > 1\) put \(V = U_t\)
3546	    (affine) and \(W = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_{t-1}\) (covered by \(t-1\) affines, hence
3547	    quasi-compact and quasi-separated). Their intersection \(W \cap V =
3548	    \bigcup_{i<t}(U_i \cap U_t)\) is covered by the \(t-1\) opens \(U_i \cap U_t\), each
3549	    an open of the affine \(U_i\) and so quasi-compact and separated; thus \(W \cap V\)
3550	    is separated and quasi-compact, and flat base change holds for it by the separated
3551	    case Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated}. The two-member cover
3552	    \(\{W, V\}\) of \(X\) gives the left-exact Mayer--Vietoris equalizer
3553	    \[
3554	      0 \to \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \to
3555	        \Gamma(W,\mathcal{F}) \oplus \Gamma(V,\mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(W\cap V,\mathcal{F}),
3556	    \]
3557	    and likewise for \(X_B\) with \(\{W_B, V_B\}\). Flatness makes \(- \otimes_A B\)
3558	    preserve this finite kernel/equalizer
3559	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}); since flat base change holds for
3560	    \(V\) (affine), for \(W\) (inductive hypothesis, \(t-1\) members), and for
3561	    \(W \cap V\) (separated), the five-lemma/equalizer comparison forces it for \(X\).
3562	  \end{proof}
3563	\end{lemma}

3565	\begin{lemma}[Reduction of the base-change map to the global-sections comparison]
3566	  \label{lem:flat_base_change_reduce_global_sections}
3567	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_isIso_iff_gammaTensorComparison}
3568	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
3569	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH (the reduction "part (1) follows from
3570	  % part (2)") (read from references/stacks-coherent.md; quoted verbatim in
3571	  % the SOURCE QUOTE PROOF of thm:flat_base_change_pushforward below).
3572	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "since $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is quasi-coherent ..., it is the
3573	  % quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_S$-module associated to the $A$-module
3574	  % $H^0(S, R^if_*\mathcal{F}) = H^i(X, \mathcal{F})$ ... Since pullback by $g$
3575	  % corresponds to $- \otimes_A B$ on modules ... we see that it suffices to
3576	  % prove (2)."
3577	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the reduction
3578	  ``part (1) follows from part (2)''.}
3579	  In the cartesian square with \(g\) flat and \(f\) quasi-compact and
3580	  quasi-separated, the sheaf-level base-change map
3581	  \(g^*(f_*\mathcal{F}) \to f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) of
3582	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism if and only if,
3583	  after reducing to the affine bases \(S = \operatorname{Spec} A\),
3584	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec} B\), the module comparison map
3585	  \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to \Gamma(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\) is an
3586	  isomorphism.
3587	  \begin{proof}
3588	    \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
3589	    Being an isomorphism is local on \(S'\), so we may take \(S = \operatorname{Spec} A\)
3590	    and \(S' = \operatorname{Spec} B\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
3591	    quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on \(S\)
3592	    (Stacks Tag 01XJ / Mathlib's quasi-coherence of the pushforward under a
3593	    quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism), hence is the sheaf associated to the
3594	    \(A\)-module \(\Gamma(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\); likewise
3595	    \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is associated to \(\Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\). Pullback along
3596	    \(g\) corresponds to \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, so under the
3597	    tilde-equivalence the sheaf base-change map is the tilde of the comparison map
3598	    \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to \Gamma(X_B,\mathcal{F}_B)\); since
3599	    \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is fully faithful on quasi-coherent modules, one is an
3600	    isomorphism iff the other is.
3601	  \end{proof}
3602	\end{lemma}
